methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $87M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Osaro is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Derik Pridmore and Michael Kahane that has raised $86.8M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates against a peer universe of 9 comparable companies (1 public, 8 private) operating in the robotics and manufacturing sector, including later-stage companies such as 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat.

Two valuation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method, using total capital raised of $87M as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B-to-later stage progression, implies a valuation of $165M. The Peer-Median method benchmarks against six same-stage, same-sector comparables, yielding a median valuation of $1.1B (with a range of $300M to $10.0B, reflecting material dispersion driven by outliers like 1X Technologies at $10B).

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $140M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $620M. This wide spread reflects substantial uncertainty in how to weight the two methods given the sparse and dispersed comparable set. The Stage-Progression method produces a more conservative anchor, while the Peer-Median is heavily influenced by a few high-valued outliers. Resolution of this range would benefit from greater visibility into Osaro's revenue, growth trajectory, and capital efficiency relative to named peers.
